definition: Approximate expensive optimal-transport distances with entropic Sinkhorn iterations, Nyström acceleration, sliced Wasserstein variants, or learned OT embeddings, then convert distances to SPECTRA similarities or nearest-neighbor edges.

scale_profile.best_for: Distribution-valued samples where bin geometry matters and simple L1/cosine distances miss transport structure.
scale_profile.typical_upper_bound: Large histogram collections when vectorized Sinkhorn, sliced projections, or OT embeddings are used.
scale_profile.avoid_when: The ground metric is not defensible or regularization would erase the shift being audited.

required_inputs: ground cost matrix or bin geometry

tunable_parameters.name: regularization
tunable_parameters.purpose: Controls the speed/fidelity tradeoff in entropic OT.

tunable_parameters.name: distance_to_similarity_scale
tunable_parameters.purpose: Controls conversion from transport distance to similarity.

quality_gates: Report the ground metric and regularization strength.
Compare with exact OT or a high-iteration reference on a subset.
Check that distance-to-similarity scaling preserves the intended spectral ordering.
failure_modes: Strong regularization can over-smooth meaningful distribution differences.
Poorly chosen ground costs make OT scientifically arbitrary.
Sinkhorn can be numerically unstable for sparse histograms without safeguards.
example_usage: python histogram_sinkhorn_topk.py --train train.csv --eval eval.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --histogram-prefix bin_ --epsilon 0.1 --top-k 50

citations.title: Sinkhorn Distances: Lightspeed Computation of Optimal Transportation Distances
citations.evidence: Introduces entropic OT solved with Sinkhorn-Knopp matrix scaling and reports large speedups over classical transport solvers.

citations.title: Sliced Gromov-Wasserstein
citations.evidence: Introduces sliced Gromov-Wasserstein as a faster alternative for comparing distributions in different metric spaces.
